protocol.name: treeland_ddm_v2
protocol.copyright: SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2025-2026 UnionTech Software Technology Co., Ltd. SPDX-License-Identifier: MIT

interface.name: treeland_ddm_v2

description.summary: core global for treeland - ddm connection

interface.description:
This is the treeland - ddm private communication protocol.
This object is primarily used to establish connection between treeland and ddm.
Warning! The protocol described in this file is currently in the testing phase. Backward compatible changes may be added together with the corresponding interface version bump. Backward incompatible changes can only be done by creating a new major version of the extension.

enum.name: capabilities

description.summary: capabilities of what ddm can perform on this machine

enum.description:
Capabilities of what ddm can perform on this machine. Treeland can adjust its behavior based on it.
See the capabilities request for how it is used.

enum.name: session_type
enum.description:
Desktop session types, X11 or Wayland.
See the login event for how it is used.

request.name: capabilities
request.description:
ddm will send its power management capabilities to treeland through this request. The argument is a bitfield of capabilities that ddm can perform on. See the capabilities enum.
This is primarily used for treeland to adjust its behavior based on what ddm can perform on this machine. For example, if ddm doesn't have poweroff capability, treeland will hide poweroff option in the UI.

request.name: user_logged_in

description.summary: send treeland the user that have already logged in

request.description:
This is primarily used for crash-recovery of treeland.
At the time the connection established, ddm will send the users and their corresponding XDG session ID that "have already logged in" by sending this request multiple times.
Users that "have already logged in" are those who already have active systemd-logind sessions, but dropped-off from the previous-connected treeland, probably due to its crash.

request.name: authentication_failed
request.description: Notify treeland about authentication failure after login or unlock.

request.name: switch_to_user

description.summary: call treeland to switch lockscreen user

request.description: Set lockscreen user to username. Barely lockscreen when username is "dde".

request.name: activate_session
request.description: Activate treeland session. This will make treeland try to take control of screen.

request.name: deactivate_session
request.description: Deactivate treeland session. This will release control of the screen, but not to close the current seats.

request.name: enable_render
request.description: Enable treeland rendering. This is primarily called after disable_render to resume treeland.

request.name: disable_render
request.description: Disable treeland rendering. This will prevent treeland from output to DRM device.

event.name: login
event.description:
Log a user in with username and secret. session_type is the desktop session type that the user wants to log in to, session_file is the .desktop file of the session.
Please listen to systemd-logind for whether the session is exactly opened or not. Failed login attempt will be notified by authentication_failed request.
Typically used when user click the login button on lockscreen.

event.name: logout
event.description:
Logout the session.
session is identical to the value of XDG_SESSION_ID, or the value of org.freedesktop.login1.Session.Id, or the value of a string returned by sd_get_sessions().
Please listen to org.freedesktop.login1.Manager.SessionRemoved signal for whether the session is exactly closed or not.
Typically used when user click the logout button in system menu.

event.name: lock
event.description:
Lock the session.
session is identical to the value of XDG_SESSION_ID, or the value of org.freedesktop.login1.Session.Id, or the value of a string returned by sd_get_sessions().
Please listen to org.freedesktop.login1.Session.Lock signal for whether the session is exactly locked or not.
Typically used when user click the lock button in system menu.

event.name: unlock
event.description:
Unlock the session with secret.
session is identical to the value of XDG_SESSION_ID, or the value of org.freedesktop.login1.Session.Id, or the value of a string returned by sd_get_sessions().
Please listen to org.freedesktop.login1.Session.Unlock signal for whether the session is exactly unlocked or not. Failed unlock attempt will be notified by authentication_failed request.
Typically used when user click the unlock button on lockscreen.

event.name: poweroff
event.description:
Poweroff the machine.
This is typically used when user click the poweroff button.

event.name: reboot
event.description:
Reboot the machine.
This is typically used when user click the reboot button.

event.name: suspend
event.description:
Suspend the machine.
This is typically used when user click the suspend button.

event.name: hibernate
event.description:
Hibernate the machine.
This is typically used when user click the hibernate button.

event.name: hybrid_sleep
event.description:
Hybrid sleep the machine.
This is typically used when user click the hybrid sleep button.

event.name: switch_to_vt
event.description: Call ddm to switch current virtual terminal to vtnr. ddm should take care of the switch and call ioctl respectively.
